notes.anchor: Tell them to promise the doctor whatever fee he asks
notes.note: The physician treating the sick Tiro at Patrae, identified elsewhere as Asclapo; Cicero authorizes an open-ended fee to secure his best care.

notes.anchor: The day of my promises is at hand
notes.note: An allusion to Cicero's promise to free Tiro: the freedman's manumission, which the Cicero brothers were arranging around this time (cf. 16.16).

notes.anchor: The third day before the Ides, at the sixth hour
notes.note: 11 April 53 BC, at midday (the sixth hour was reckoned from sunrise); written from Cicero's villa near Cumae.
